title: Coffea fontanesii
intro: Coffea fontanesii, also known as the Yemen coffee tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the highlands of Yemen. The leaves and fruits of Coffea fontanesii have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headaches, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Coffea fontanesii include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coffea fontanesii include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Coffea fontanesii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Coffea, which consists of around 125 species of coffee plants. Coffea fontanesii is native to Madagascar and is closely related to the Arabica coffee plant. The species is further classified as being part of the Coffea species that are evergreen shrubs or small trees. Coffea fontanesii is also known for its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native environment.
